title: Indulgent Japanese Egg Sushi
description: A buttery, creamy egg sushi with a subtle ginger glaze, perfect for ovo-vegetarians.
instructions: Rinse sushi rice until water runs clear, then combine 200 g rice with 250 ml water in a saucepan, bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes.
Remove saucepan from heat, let rice steam covered for 10 minutes, then fluff with a bamboo paddle.
In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sugar, and 1 tsp salt until sugar dissolves, then fold into the warm rice.

Whisk 4 large eggs in a bowl, pour into a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat, cook for 5 minutes while stirring gently, then transfer to a plate and let cool to 70°C before slicing into 8 equal strips.
Melt 30 g butter in a small saucepan, add 10 g grated ginger, and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Turn off heat and stir in 2 tbsp soy sauce and 2 tbsp mirin into the butter‑ginger sauce, letting it cool slightly.
Wet hands with water, shape a small mound of seasoned rice into a flat oval, then gently press a slice of egg on top, forming a nigiri.
Repeat step 7 for the remaining rice mounds and egg slices.
Brush each nigiri with the ginger‑butter glaze, arranging them on a plate.
Serve immediately, accompanied by extra glaze for drizzling.
